creator: pirzyk@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Carlo Strub from comment #4)
> Sorry, I don't understand. The last entry in this Changelog says for example:
> 
>   * FreeBSD
>     + Support for FreeBSD7.
>     + Support for sendmail 8.14
>     + Support all ifconfig options in the network_interfaces directive.
> 
> So, it seems to me that FreeBSD is supported. Anyway. You -- of course --
> have also the option to send this port back to the pool and give up
> maintainership. But, I have still doubts giving up this port entirely.
> 
> Any comments?

Yes, that ChangeLog comment is for 3.3, not for 0.8.5 which you are going to downgrade the port to.  The *first* reference to FreeBSD in the ChangeLog is for version 0.9.dev0.

You will *break* the port if you downgrade to 0.8.5 and that is the most recent version you can find source tarballs for.
